data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-09T01:46:27.633Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating a .NET 10 web API to .NET 11. The app uses EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider.
We have a repository pattern where some methods use synchronous LINQ:

```csharp
public class ProductRepository
{
    private readonly CosmosDbContext _context;

    public List<Product> GetAll()
    {
        return _context.Products.ToList();
    }

    public Product GetById(int id)
    {
        return _context.Products.First(p => p.Id == id);
    }

    public int GetCount()
    {
        return _context.Products.Count();
    }

    public void Save(Product product)
    {
        _context.Products.Add(product);
        _context.SaveChanges();
    }
}
```

In .NET 10 we had the sync-over-async opt-in configured. What breaks in .NET 11?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about what breaks when migrating from .NET 10 to .NET 11 with EF Core using the Azure Cosmos DB provider, specifically around synchronous LINQ operations.

This is a known breaking change in EF Core. Let me explain what I know:

In EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider, synchronous operations were never natively supported — they were always implemented as sync-over-async internally. In EF Core 10 (.NET 10), there was an opt-in mechanism to allow this pattern. In .NET 11 (EF Core 11), this sync-over-async support is being removed entirely.


The progression is clear: EF Core 9 deprecated synchronous I/O on the Cosmos provider, EF Core 10 made it throw by default but allowed you to opt back in through the Cosmos configuration, and EF Core 11 removes that opt-in entirely with no fallback available.

The solution is straightforward—I need to convert all the synchronous LINQ operations like `ToList()`, `First()`, and `SaveChanges()` to their async counterparts: `ToListAsync()`, `FirstAsync()`, and `SaveChangesAsync()`.
